title: Fish in Danger: Protecting Migratory Fish
summary: Fish that travel long distances in rivers and lakes are in big trouble. Their numbers have dropped by 81% in the last 50 years. This is because of dams, pollution, and too much fishing. These fish cannot reach their breeding places. The United Nations (UN) made a new report to help protect them. They picked 30 special fish, like the golden mahseer and goonch catfish, to save first. These fish are important for people because they provide food and are part of culture. For example, the golden mahseer is called the tiger of the river in the Himalayas. Scientists say we need to stop building dams and overfishing to help these fish survive.

questions.question: What should we stop to help migratory fish?

background_read: Migratory fish are fish that swim long distances in freshwater like rivers and lakes to find food or breed. They face many problems like dams that block their way, pollution that makes water dirty, and people catching too many fish. The United Nations is a global organization that helps protect animals and the environment. In this article, they made a report to save 30 special fish species that are in danger. These fish are not just for food; they are also important in cultures around the world, like the golden mahseer in the Himalayas. Learning about them helps us understand why we need to take care of our rivers and lakes.
Article_Structure: WHO: The United Nations and scientists studied migratory fish.
WHAT: Migratory fish numbers dropped by 81% due to dams, pollution, and overfishing.
WHERE: This happens in rivers and lakes around the world, like in the Himalayas.
WHY: To protect these fish because they are important for food and culture.

perspectives.description: People want to save migratory fish by stopping dams and overfishing to help them survive and breed.
